data.title: Bitcoin Surpasses 67,000 USDT Mark
data.description: According to Foresight News, Bitcoin has risen above 67,000 USDT, currently trading at 67,012.12 USDT. This marks a daily increase of 1.10%.

data.title: BlackRock's global head of digital assets denies that "IBIT hedge fund liquidation caused the sharp market decline."
data.description: Robert Mitchnick, Global Head of Digital Assets at BlackRock, stated that institutions, sovereign wealth funds, and banks are currently buying Bitcoin on dips. Regarding market rumors that the recent sharp market decline was due to IBIT hedge funds liquidating and dumping Bitcoin, Mitchnick said, "There's a misconception that hedge funds are maliciously dumping Bitcoin in ETFs to create volatility, but the data completely contradicts this claim. During last week's sharp Bitcoin correction, BlackRock IBIT saw only 0.2% net redemptions. If it were truly a large-scale hedge fund liquidation for arbitrage, there should have been billions of dollars in outflows. The reality is that liquidations were concentrated on leveraged perpetual contract platforms; IBIT's funding situation is extremely stable, and its holders are primarily long-term portfolio investors."

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Iraq's Internal Affairs: Former Prime Minister Nouri al-Maliki's Reappointment Decision
data.description: Iraq's Foreign Minister has stated that the decision to reappoint former Prime Minister Nouri al-Maliki, who is now serving as the deputy prime minister, is an internal matter for Iraq. According to Jin10, the minister emphasized that Iraq is paying close attention to signals from the United States regarding this decision. The reappointment has sparked discussions about its implications for Iraq's political landscape and its relations with the U.S. The move is seen as a significant development in Iraq's domestic politics, highlighting the country's focus on maintaining diplomatic ties while navigating internal governance issues.

data.title: Trina Solar Faces Regulatory Warning Over Misleading SpaceX Collaboration Claims
data.description: Trina Solar Co., Ltd. has received a regulatory warning from the Shanghai Stock Exchange due to misleading claims about a collaboration with SpaceX. According to Jin10, the company had previously indicated on the Shanghai Stock Exchange's E-Interaction platform that it was engaged in a partnership with SpaceX, which was found to be false. As a result, the exchange has issued a supervisory warning to Trina Solar and its former board secretary, Wu Qun, for the violation. The announcement underscores the importance of accurate corporate communications and adherence to regulatory standards.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 67,000 USDT with a Narrowed 0.56% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 13, 2026, 09:48 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 67,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 67,015.539063 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 0.56% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Chinese Banks Report Decline in Foreign Exchange Conversion to Yuan in January
data.description: Chinese banks experienced a decrease in foreign-exchange conversions into the yuan in January. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the trend as clients opted for fewer conversions compared to previous months. This shift comes amid ongoing economic adjustments and currency fluctuations. Analysts suggest that the decline may be influenced by various factors, including market conditions and investor sentiment. The reduction in conversions reflects broader economic dynamics and potential impacts on China's financial landscape.

data.title: BlackRock Increases Investment in Bitmine Amid Ethereum Price Decline
data.description: BlackRock has significantly boosted its investment in Bitmine, a leading company focused on Ethereum, despite a nearly 60% drop in Ethereum's price from its peak. According to NS3.AI, this move comes as the market experiences weakness, leading to substantial sell-offs by industry pioneers and a sharp decline in Bitmine's share price. However, major Wall Street firms, including Goldman Sachs and BlackRock, are taking advantage of the dip to increase their holdings. BlackRock remains confident in Ethereum's future, highlighting its strong position in tokenized asset ecosystems and its continued leadership in blockchain innovation.

data.title: Ares Collaborates with Lenders for Debt Package
data.description: Ares Management Corp. is joining forces with financial institutions such as Park Square Capital and Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp. to offer a comprehensive debt package. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic collaboration aimed at enhancing financial solutions. This partnership underscores Ares' commitment to expanding its lending capabilities by leveraging the expertise and resources of its partners. The initiative is expected to provide significant support to borrowers, aligning with Ares' broader financial strategies.

data.title: Aave Labs plans to request a $50 million grant to allocate revenue from various products to its DAO.
data.description: Aave Labs has launched a Temp Check proposal to token holders, requesting approximately $50 million in funding in exchange for all revenue from Aave-branded products being allocated to the Aave DAO treasury. The proposal includes up to $42.5 million in stablecoins (a $25 million base grant and $17.5 million in milestone rewards) and 75,000 AAVE tokens (approximately $8 million). In return, Aave Labs will allocate 100% of the revenue from all its products, including aave.com, the planned Aave Card, and Aave Pro, to the DAO. This proposal has raised concerns within the community about the concentration of voting power. Marc Zeller, founder of the Aave Chan Initiative, pointed out that the package represents a significant proportion of the DAO treasury and questioned whether the 75,000 AAVE token grant could lead to excessive concentration of voting power, calling for the proposal to be split and for recipients to disclose their holding addresses. Aave founder Stani Kulechov stated that this move will shift DAOs towards a "token-centric" model, enhancing their ability to fund growth and execute buybacks. The proposal is currently in the public comment phase. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: IMF Highlights Concerns Over India's Data Methodology
data.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has raised concerns regarding the methodology used in India's data collection. Bloomberg posted on X that in December, the IMF identified certain methodological weaknesses in India's data, which it believes could hinder effective surveillance. The IMF's observations suggest that improvements in data collection and analysis are necessary to enhance the accuracy and reliability of economic assessments. These concerns highlight the importance of robust data practices for informed policy-making and economic monitoring.

data.title: Hospitality in Hong Kong Praised by Industry Insider
data.description: Jack Kong, CEO of Nano Labs, posted on X, expressing appreciation for the hospitality experienced in Hong Kong. The post highlighted the positive interactions and welcoming atmosphere encountered during a recent visit. The sentiment was echoed by industry peers, emphasizing the city's reputation for excellent hospitality. The visit is seen as a promising step towards future collaborations and engagements within the region.

data.title: STOCKS | Apple's Stock Declines Amid Siri Upgrade Delay and Regulatory Pressure
data.description: Apple's stock experienced a 5% decline after reports surfaced about a delay in the anticipated Siri upgrade due to performance issues. According to NS3.AI, the situation was further compounded by increased regulatory scrutiny, as the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) accused Apple of biased content moderation within its Apple News segment. These developments have contributed to a bearish sentiment among investors, who remain uncertain about the company's near-term prospects.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Bank of America Analyst Discusses Impact of U.S. Trade Policies
data.description:
Bank of America's Michael Hartnett has highlighted the significant impact of U.S. trade policies on the global economic landscape. Bloomberg posted on X that Hartnett described these policies as instrumental in shaping a 'new world order.'

Hartnett's analysis suggests that the current trade strategies employed by the United States are leading to substantial shifts in international economic relations. These changes are influencing global markets and altering traditional trade dynamics.

The commentary from Hartnett underscores the broader implications of U.S. trade policies, which are seen as pivotal in redefining economic alliances and power structures worldwide. As these policies continue to evolve, their effects on the global economy remain a critical area of focus for analysts and policymakers alike.

data.title: Cologne-Bonn Airport Suspends Operations Due to Security Incident
data.description: Cologne-Bonn Airport in western Germany temporarily halted operations on February 13 due to a security incident. According to Jin10, a spokesperson for the Cologne police stated that a malfunction in a luggage scanner necessitated additional security checks for many passengers. However, further details were not disclosed by the police. Additionally, the German Federal Police informed the media that the timeline for resuming airport operations remains uncertain.

data.title: South African Reserve Bank Expected to Cut Interest Rates Further
data.description: The South African Reserve Bank is anticipated to reduce interest rates three more times before concluding its current easing cycle, according to a recent survey. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the central bank's strategy to stimulate economic growth amid challenging conditions. The survey suggests that these rate cuts are part of a broader effort to support the economy, which has been facing various pressures. The Reserve Bank's approach aligns with global trends where central banks are adjusting monetary policies to foster economic stability. The anticipated rate reductions are expected to provide relief to businesses and consumers, potentially boosting spending and investment. However, the exact timing and magnitude of these cuts remain uncertain, as they will depend on evolving economic indicators and conditions. The Reserve Bank's decisions will be closely monitored by market participants and analysts, who are keen to understand the implications for the South African economy.

data.title: East Asia Bank's Interim Dividend Declines Amid One-Time Factors
data.description:
East Asia Bank announced a second interim dividend of HK$0.22 per share, marking a 42% decrease from the previous year. According to RTHK, Co-Chief Executive Li Man Bun attributed the reduced dividend to one-time factors, including losses from mainland joint ventures and associates. He expressed optimism that as these impacts dissipate, profitability will return to normal, leading to a rebound in dividends.

Last year, East Asia's net interest margin narrowed by 19 basis points to 1.9%. Co-Chief Executive Li Min Qiao noted that the margin improved in the latter half of the year. He anticipates that the U.S. Federal Reserve may cut interest rates in June and September, but expects loan demand to rise, maintaining a stable margin in the first half of the year.

Li Min Qiao highlighted the bank's proactive efforts to develop non-interest income last year, which helped offset declines in net interest income. Looking ahead, the bank plans to capitalize on corporate expansion overseas and the growing demand for wealth management services to drive profit growth.

As of the end of last year, East Asia Bank's total workforce stood at 7,669, a reduction of 192 employees since June, with 112 fewer staff in Hong Kong. Li Min Qiao stated that there are no plans for layoffs this year. Instead, the bank will continue leveraging technology to enhance operational efficiency and is actively recruiting personnel such as client relationship managers.

data.description:
